page.title: Thyroid Cancer (Well-Differentiated) — RepurpOS | OpenSourceMedicine
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for Thyroid Cancer: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for Thyroid Cancer: 189 alterations and 321 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Not applicable — surgical resection is standard; papillary microcarcinoma (<1 cm, low-risk): active surveillance achieves 99.8%+ cancer-specific survival at 10 years without immediate surgery in Japanese cohorts
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Total thyroidectomy + RAI for intermediate/high-risk: 90-95% disease-free survival at 10 years; active surveillance for low-risk papillary microcarcinoma: non-inferior to surgery in multiple cohorts; lenvatinib + pembrolizumab for RAI-refractory: OS benefit (LEAP-011 ongoing); vemurafenib for BRAF V600E mutant: response in 38%
remission.gap_size: Moderate — overtreatment of low-risk disease is the dominant problem (too many total thyroidectomies in low-risk disease)
remission.barrier_detail: The Kuma Hospital and Cancer Institute Hospital experience in Japan showing non-inferiority of active surveillance for low-risk papillary microcarcinoma has not been widely adopted outside Japan; total thyroidectomy with radioiodine for all differentiated thyroid cancer remains common despite lack of benefit in low-risk disease
remission.last_soc_change: 2023 — ATA task force expanded active surveillance indications; lenvatinib now standard second-line; 2018 ATA guidelines reduced RAI use in low-risk; total thyroidectomy + RAI standard since 1990s (largely unchanged)
remission.outdated_treatments: Total thyroidectomy + RAI for all papillary thyroid microcarcinomas (overtreatment; active surveillance non-inferior in low-risk); TSH suppression to very low levels in low-risk patients (increases cardiovascular and bone risk without cancer benefit); routine post-thyroidectomy calcium supplementation without individualised assessment
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Active surveillance for low-risk PTMC (expanding from Japan to US/Europe); selenium supplementation for Hashimoto's co-existing with differentiated thyroid cancer (reduces anti-TPO, may improve treatment tolerance); exercise during thyroid cancer treatment
remission.frontier_signal: BRAF/MEK inhibitors for advanced BRAF V600E papillary thyroid cancer (dabrafenib + trametinib — FDA approved 2018 for other cancers, compassionate use thyroid cancer); RET inhibitors (selpercatinib, pralsetinib) for RET-mutant medullary thyroid cancer; NTRK inhibitors (larotrectinib) for NTRK-fusion thyroid cancers
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.

burden.note: Tier from NIH FY funding ÷ US DALYs (or mortality proxy) vs corpus quartiles
